issuesFound.title: Readability concern due to small base font size

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: Setting a negative word-spacing value causes words to be crowded together. This significantly degrades the reading experience for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: Negative letter-spacing reduces the space between individual characters, making them harder to distinguish. This is a barrier for users with visual impairments or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 169: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in high-contrast mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query explicitly removes the focus outline. In high-contrast mode, users rely on system-defined colors for visibility; removing the outline entirely blocks keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class and body scroll lock but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1458: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function changes the layout mode between grid and list views but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the buttons. Users of assistive technology will not know which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true'); // and set 'false' on siblings`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module updates the error message text and visibility but fails to set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not perceive the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3018: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation in the `_renderResult` function, the `aria-invalid` attribute is not reset to `false` or removed. A field previously marked as invalid will continue to be announced as invalid even after the user corrects the data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3013: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not explicitly set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger. If the default HTML is missing this attribute, the initial state is undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge` function updates the visible text of the product count but does not trigger a live region announcement. Users who cannot see the badge will not know how many products remain after a filter is applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2345: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count));`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class on the drawer but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not receive feedback that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` Line 1158: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function switches the product grid between 'grid' and 'list' modes but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the buttons. Users of assistive technology will not know which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1845: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter is applied, the `_updateCountBadge` function updates the visible text of the count badge, but it does not write to an ARIA live region. Blind users will not be informed of how many products remain after filtering.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module applies an invalid CSS class and shows an error message but does not set the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute on the input field. Assistive technologies will not programmatically perceive the field as being in an error state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2840: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 2849: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function updates visual classes for period buttons but does not set aria-pressed to indicate which button is selected. Screen reader users cannot perceive the active billing period, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4240: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product count and grid but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change, failing WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3654: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : \`${totalMatches} products found\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Price change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates all plan card prices but does not announce the billing period change via a live region. Screen reader users may not know that prices have updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4218: After `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function adds error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid on the field. Screen reader users may not be notified of the invalid state, failing WCAG 4.1.1 (Parsing) and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5120: In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`. In the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set an initial aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button. Screen reader users may not know the button controls a collapsible menu on page load, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: After `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup but does not set an initial aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons. Screen reader users are not informed of the initial collapsed state, failing WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3040: After `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close functions change visible state but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users won't know if the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In mobileNav.close function, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown open/close functions show/hide panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance function, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In _closeInstance function, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product view mode toggle does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The grid/list view toggle changes layout but does not set aria-pressed on the toggle buttons. Screen reader users cannot tell which view is active.
issuesFound.fix: In _onViewToggle function, after setting _productFilterState.viewMode, add: qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode ? 'true' : 'false'); });

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown/hidden, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed about the number of results or filter changes.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters function, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods (month/year) updates price displays but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod function, after _updateSavingsMessage, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav aria-expanded not initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized when the page loads. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init function, after _trigger is assigned, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu aria-expanded not initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded initialized when the page loads. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init function, after registering each dropdown, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list updates without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When search suggestions are rendered via innerHTML, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that new suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: In suggestionListRenderer.render function, after setting innerHTML, add: if (items.length) { liveRegion.announce(`${items.length} suggestion${items.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} available`); }

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer does not trap focus when open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped inside it. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background, breaking modal interaction.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open function, after adding classes, add: _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true }); _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel does not trap focus when open
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel opens, focus is not trapped inside it. Keyboard users can tab out of the dropdown, breaking the interaction pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance function, after removing hidden attribute, add: const trap = createFocusTrap(inst.panel, { escapeDeactivates: true }); trap.activate(); inst._trap = trap;

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module’s period‑selection buttons (elements with [data‑period]) are toggled with CSS classes only. No aria‑pressed attribute is set, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (SELECTORS.searchInput) is given aria‑autocomplete, aria‑controls, aria‑haspopup etc., but no <label or aria‑label is provided. Without an accessible name, screen‑reader users cannot discover the purpose of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3405:   setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', 'Search');   // add after the element is retrieved

issuesFound.title: Site‑search combobox does not announce open/close state via aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input functions as a combobox (listbox of suggestions) but never updates aria‑expanded when the suggestion panel opens or closes. This prevents assistive technology from conveying the listbox visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3412:   setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');   // add inside _open()
    Line 3420:   setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');  // add inside _close()

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult adds error text and CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid on the field when validation fails. Screen‑readers therefore cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5608:   if (!result.valid) { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); }   // add inside the if (!result.valid) branch
    Line 5620:   else { field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); }                     // add inside the else branch (valid case)

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously‑invalid field is corrected, the validator removes error text but does not remove the aria‑invalid attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5620:   else { field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); }   // ensure aria‑invalid is cleared on the valid path (as shown above)

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle does not provide an accessible name for the buttons

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The “hamburger” button (`_trigger`) opens and closes the mobile drawer, but the script never sets `aria‑expanded` on the button. Assistive technologies therefore cannot know whether the navigation is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` but its pressed state is never reflected with `aria‑pressed`. Screen readers cannot convey the current period (monthly vs. annual) to users.

issuesFound.title: Suggestion list options do not update aria‑selected when highlighted
issuesFound.explanation: When navigating the autocomplete list with the keyboard, the script adds a visual “active” class but never updates `aria‑selected` on the `<li role="option">` elements. Assistive technologies therefore cannot identify the currently highlighted suggestion.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields (or clear it on valid fields)
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult` only adds/removes visual error classes and error‑message elements. It never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on an invalid field nor removes the attribute when the field becomes valid, so screen readers are unaware of validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:active` selector applies a `transform: translateY(1px)` animation on activation, but there is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links, relying solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or provide an alternative visual indicator (e.g., `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }`).

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px for body text

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onPointerDown` and `_onPointerUp` event handlers in the `testimonialsCarousel` module are attached to a non-native interactive element (the carousel root). These handlers respond to pointer events but do not include matching keyboard handlers for Enter and Space keys, which violates WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.1.1 Keyboard. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot trigger the swipe functionality.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle uses pointerdown/pointerup/touchstart/touchend events to detect swipe gestures, but does not provide matching keydown/keyup handlers for Enter and Space keys. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot activate the swipe-to-close functionality.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close functions emit custom events but do not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers are unaware the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10235: After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });` add:
```
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
```
    Line 10245: After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });` add:
```
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');
```

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions toggle the CSS class and hidden attribute but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10235: After `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` add:
```
setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
```
    Line 10245: After `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` add:
```
setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button at page load. Screen readers may announce the button as expanded when it is actually closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10275: After `if (!_trigger || !_drawer) {` add:
```
setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator bind() function adds error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen readers cannot announce the error state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator clearField() function removes error classes and messages but does not clear aria-invalid="true" from fields. Screen readers continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav._onTriggerClick` function (lines 10235–10243) toggles the mobile navigation drawer but only listens for click events. Keyboard users who activate the trigger button using Enter or Space will not trigger the drawer because the function lacks corresponding keydown/keyup handlers. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) and 2.5.3 (Label in Name).
issuesFound.fix:
Add keydown event listeners to the trigger button to handle Enter and Space keys. Ensure the handler prevents default browser behavior for Space to avoid page scrolling.
  Line 10235: `function _onTriggerClick() {`
  Line 10235: `function _onTriggerClick(e) {`
  Line 10236: `  if (e.type === 'keydown' && e.key !== KEYS.ENTER && e.key !== KEYS.SPACE) return;`
  Line 10237: `  if (e.type === 'keydown') e.preventDefault();`
  Line 10238: `  toggle();`
  Line 10239: `}`
  Line 10240: `_trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);`
  Line 10241: `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerClick);`

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto. This fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible, effectively rendering the utility useless and potentially cluttering the visual interface with helper text intended only for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining an alternative visible focus style (such as box-shadow, border, or outline-color) in this rule. This removes the default focus indicator for all elements relying on this base rule, making keyboard navigation invisible for users who do not have component-specific overrides.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a visible focus color or shadow in this rule. Unless a utility class like .focus-ring-brand is added to every button, keyboard users will not see which button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 525: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on header logo link
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a visible focus color. This hides the focus state for the site logo/home link, hindering keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0. While #search-input:focus has a box-shadow, the :focus-visible rule overrides the outline without ensuring the shadow persists or is visible in all states, risking invisible focus for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a visible focus color. This makes footer links inaccessible to keyboard users who rely on focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1415: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none. This actively disables the system's high-contrast focus indicator, making focus invisible for users relying on Windows High Contrast or similar modes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element has a transition on transform and box-shadow (Line 735). The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (Line 745) is empty and does not disable the transition, causing motion for users who requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 745: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word spacing reduces the space between words, which can significantly impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision, violating WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative letter spacing reduces space between characters, hindering readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Base font size too small for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px. Since body text uses 1rem, the base body text size is 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) baseline for readability, causing strain for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 165: html { font-size: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger buttons

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border-color. Keyboard users navigating through the page will not be able to see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the outline on :focus-visible without providing a high-contrast alternative. Users in Windows High Contrast Mode will lose focus indicators entirely.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the default outline but does not add a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Button components will not show focus indicators for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on header logo link
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector removes outline without providing an alternative visual indicator.

issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and does not clip or hide content visually, meaning the content remains visible. Conversely, if intended to hide content, it fails to remove it from the visual flow while keeping it for AT. The current implementation makes content visible to all users.

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transition properties that affect transform and box-shadow, which can cause motion sensitivity issues. The empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block does not disable these transitions.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint class uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) which provides insufficient contrast against the white background (2.9:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a gradient background of approximately #5588bb, resulting in a contrast ratio below 3:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer text
issuesFound.explanation: The footer uses color: var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) on background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.0:1, failing WCAG AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer brand text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand > p uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on #555555 background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links use color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on #555555 background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative visual differentiator such as border-bottom, font-weight change, or sufficient color contrast difference from surrounding text. This makes it difficult for users to identify links, especially those with color vision deficiencies.

issuesFound.title: Font size below recommended minimum for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which is below the 16px (1rem) recommended for body text to ensure readability for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Line height below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height: 1, which is below the 1.5 recommended by WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation for readability.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons open and close panels, but aria-expanded is never set on the trigger element to communicate the visibility state of the dropdown to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1953: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`
    Line 1965: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox with a suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is never toggled on the input to indicate when the suggestion panel is visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2203: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`
    Line 2213: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (month/year) function as toggle buttons but do not set aria-pressed to indicate which period is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2503: Insert `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, invalid fields are not marked with aria-invalid="true" and valid fields do not have aria-invalid cleared to "false". Screen readers cannot identify which fields have errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1205: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` in markInvalid after `addDescribedBy(control, errorEl);`
    Line 1215: Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` in markValid after removing aria-describedby

issuesFound.title: breadcrumbHelpers missing aria-current implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The function contains a comment indicating it should mark the last breadcrumb item as current page, but the implementation code is missing. The last item lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1702: Insert `if (lastLink) { lastLink.setAttribute('aria-current', 'page'); } else { lastItem.querySelector('span')?.setAttribute('aria-current', 'page'); }` after the comment "// Mark the last item as the current page."

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: On page load, the mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set, even though the menu starts in a closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1785: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the initial aria-expanded="false" is not set on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1995: Insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input does not have aria-expanded="false" initially set.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2255: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons do not have aria-pressed set to reflect the initial selected period on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2555: Insert `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, btn.getAttribute('data-period') === initPeriod); });` after `const initPeriod = ...`

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but screen reader users are not notified of the results via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2115: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2305: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` at end of open function
    Line 2315: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` at end of close function

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop missing completion announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users activate the scroll-to-top button, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves, but there is no announcement confirming the action completed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1755: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');` after `scrollToY(0);`

issuesFound.title: pricing missing price change announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2525: Insert `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);` after `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons do not have aria-pressed set to reflect the current theme on initial page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2805: Insert `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current); });` after `_apply(_resolve(_current));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer declares a focus trap variable `_trap` but never initialises it with createFocusTrap, and never activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard focus is not trapped within the open drawer.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1792: Insert `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` after `_initialised = true;`
    Line 1812: Insert `_trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();` in open function

fpTitles: themeSwitcher missing initial aria-pressed state

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels but fails to update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2300 (in _openInstance): Add after `inst.open = true;`:
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line 2315 (in _closeInstance): Add after `inst.open = false;`:
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion items open and close but the trigger buttons do not have their aria-expanded attribute updated to reflect the state change, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3100 (in faqAccordion.open): Add after `item.open = true;`:
  `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line 3125 (in faqAccordion.close): Add after `item.open = false;`:
  `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons indicate active state visually via CSS classes but lack aria-pressed attributes, making the active filter unidentifiable to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3750 (in _syncTabAriaStates): Replace the forEach body with:
  `const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap created but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module creates a focus trap (createFocusTrap) stored in _trap, but the open() function never calls _trap.activate(). Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile menu into the background content, losing focus management.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2150 (in mobileNav.open): Add after `_lockScroll();`:
  `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the markInvalid function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields programmatically.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1200 (in markInvalid): Add after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`:
  `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but leaves aria-invalid="true" set, causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1205 (in markValid): Add before the closing brace:
  `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the module initializes, leaving screen readers unaware of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2180 (in mobileNav.init): Add after `_initialised = true;`:
  `if (_trigger && !_trigger.getAttribute(ARIA.EXPANDED)) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce dynamic updates
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown/hidden, the visible count changes but is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the filter results have updated or how many items are now visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3700 (in applyFilters): Add inside the filterAnimation.run callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`:
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied.replace('{count}', totalMatches) : \`Showing \${totalMatches} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled and prices update dynamically, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices have changed or what the new prices are.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3450 (in setPeriod): Add after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`:
  `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialised with aria-expanded="false". While the panel is hidden, the trigger state is not communicated to assistive technologies, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` in the register function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge text is updated but not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the results have changed or how many items match the current filters, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` at the end of the _updateCountBadge function after updating the text content.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually but are not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices have changed or what the new values are, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);` at the end of the setPeriod function after the animation frame reset.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer traps focus and blocks interaction with the rest of the page but lacks aria-modal="true". Screen readers may not treat it as a modal dialog, potentially allowing virtual cursor navigation outside the drawer, violating WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` in the open function when the drawer is opened, and remove it in the close function.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words by 20%. Negative word-spacing compresses text and impairs readability for users with dyslexia, low vision, or reading difficulties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 191: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed globally without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Links in body text are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Users with colour vision deficiencies may not perceive links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; } (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector sets color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) which is white at 30% opacity. Against the gradient background (#5588bb to #bb88ee defined in :root), this results in a contrast ratio well below 4.5:1. Text is nearly invisible for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1109-1113: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Transform animation on buttons without reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, and .btn:active applies transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447-453: Add after line 453: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Transform animation on hero image without effective reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform transitions and hover state transforms. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 724-729 contains empty rules that do not disable the transform animation. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D rotation effect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 724-729: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed on FAQ accordion buttons without visible replacement
Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links without visible replacement
Paragraph line-height set to 1 impairs readability
Transform animation on hero image without effective reduced-motion override

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments or using touch devices may struggle to activate this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 425: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto — the opposite of a proper visually-hidden utility. Content with this class will be fully visible in the layout instead of hidden visually while remaining accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended purpose of screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 298-307: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words. Negative word-spacing can make text harder to read, particularly for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 107: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% white) on a gradient background of #5588bb to #bb88ee. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.4-1.6:1, far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read this text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 823: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links globally. Links in body text are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour. There is no .prose a rule to restore underlines in content areas.
issuesFound.fix: Line 131: a { text-decoration: none; } .prose a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast);) but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable or reduce this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience transform animations on button interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 313-315: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { html { scroll-behavior: auto; } .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation has empty prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform and box-shadow transitions, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rules for .hero-image img and .hero-image img:hover are empty and do not disable the motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D perspective transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447-452: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.title: Base font-size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, making 1rem equal to 12px. Since body uses font-size: var(--font-size-base) (1rem), body text renders at 12px, below the recommended 16px minimum for readability. Users with low vision or reading difficulties may struggle with this text size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 103: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 16px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Line-height below recommended minimum for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height: 1, which is below the recommended 1.5 minimum for readable body text. Single-line height makes paragraphs difficult to read, especially for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 122: p { line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); text-wrap: pretty; }

fpTitles: Motion animation has empty prefers-reduced-motion override
Base font-size below recommended minimum
Line-height below recommended minimum for body text

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load. Assistive technology users may not know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2768: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the code ensures aria-haspopup is set but does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" on the trigger button. Assistive technology users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2935: `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing.init() does not initialize aria-pressed on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element does not have aria-pressed initialized on page load. If the toggle element is not a native button, it needs role="switch" and aria-checked; if it is a button, it needs aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4680: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as invalid, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen reader users will not be notified that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1981: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as valid, the function removes the aria-describedby reference but never clears aria-invalid. Screen reader users may continue to hear that the field is invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1994: `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When products are filtered, the visible content changes but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the product list has been updated or how many products match the filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3860: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || `Showing ${s.activeCategory} products`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed, prices update visually but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the prices have changed to monthly or annual billing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4582: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not set aria-expanded on trigger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide panels and toggle CSS classes, but never set aria-expanded on the trigger element. The register() function initializes aria-haspopup and aria-controls but not aria-expanded, leaving dropdown state inaccessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2393: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2401: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`. In register() around line 2435, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the panel.setAttribute('hidden', '') line.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid on fields with errors
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the field to its error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid. Similarly, markValid() does not clear aria-invalid when the field becomes valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1674: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after the addDescribedBy call. In markValid() at line 1680, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after the aria-describedby cleanup.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge() function updates the visible count text and aria-label, but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the number of visible products changes after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3165: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(String(count)) : \`Showing \${count} product\${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}\`);` after the setAttr call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes and the hidden attribute on the drawer/overlay but never set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users cannot tell whether the navigation menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2747: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_lockScroll();`. Line 2762: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_unlockScroll();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not trap focus or return focus on close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares `let _trap = null;` but never assigns it a focus trap. When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, and when it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users can tab out of the modal and become disoriented.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750: add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true }); _trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`. Line 2770: change `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();` to `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }` and add `if (_savedFocus) _savedFocus.focus({ preventScroll: true });` where `_savedFocus` is captured in open() as `_savedFocus = document.activeElement;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not move focus into panel on open or return focus on close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens via keyboard (Enter/Space/Arrow), focus is not moved to the first focusable item in the panel. When closed via Escape, focus is not returned to the trigger despite the returnFocus parameter existing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2945: add `requestAnimationFrame(() => { const first = getFocusableChildren(inst.panel)[0]; if (first) first.focus(); });` after the panel is shown. Line 2955: add `if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });` before the emit in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function toggles the CSS active class on filter buttons but does not set aria-pressed="true"/"false". Filter buttons act as toggle controls and must convey their pressed state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3598: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function toggles CSS classes for the billing period toggle but does not set aria-checked on the element with role="switch". For a switch role, aria-checked is required to indicate the on/off state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3961: add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));` inside the `if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack)` block.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds aria-describedby to associate the field with its error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1627: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false". The field remains marked as invalid in the accessibility tree even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1633: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` at the start of the function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce filter changes
issuesFound.explanation: When products are filtered, the visible count changes and cards are shown/hidden, but there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3589: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(activeCategory) || 'Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');` after `_updateEmptyState(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggle switches between monthly and annual, prices update visually but there is no live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3938: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));` after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without an aria-label prop. Icon-only buttons must have an accessible name to convey their purpose to screen reader users. The IconButton interface requires aria-label, but this instance omits it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1060: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav submenu trigger button has aria-controls pointing to the submenu, but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the submenu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 980: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The FilterTabs filter buttons use visual styling to indicate active state but lack aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The NewsletterForm email input has aria-describedby referencing "newsletter-email-error" when invalid, but no element with that id exists in the DOM. The FormGroup component only renders ErrorMessage when errorId prop is passed, which is not done here.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2410: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Carousel slides missing aria-hidden when inactive
issuesFound.explanation: TestimonialsCarousel renders all slides without aria-hidden on inactive slides. Screen readers will announce all slides simultaneously instead of only the visible slide, violating the carousel pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: aria-modal placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the overlay div instead of the dialog div with role="dialog". aria-modal must be on the element with role="dialog" to properly signal modality to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2845: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Fix: Line 2850: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: Repeated nav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks nav element is one of multiple nav landmarks on the page but lacks aria-label or aria-labelledby to distinguish it for screen reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 730: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: Section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection section element lacks aria-labelledby pointing to its heading. When multiple sections exist, each should have an accessible name for screen reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: Loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component renders a spinner span with class "btn-spinner" during loading without aria-hidden="true". This decorative element may be announced by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 530: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component does not communicate its loading state programmatically via aria-busy when isLoading is true. Screen reader users cannot detect that the button is in a busy state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Spinner decorative element missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The Spinner component renders a decorative "spinner__ring" span without aria-hidden="true". While the parent has role="status" and aria-label, the decorative ring should be hidden from assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: MobileNav toggle button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger menu toggle button has aria-controls and an aria-label that changes based on state, but is missing aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state programmatically to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation landmark uses id="main-nav" but lacks an aria-label. With multiple navigation regions on the page (skip links, desktop nav, mobile nav, footer nav), each should have a distinguishing accessible name.

issuesFound.explanation: The email input's aria-describedby attribute references "newsletter-email-error" when there is an error, but no element with that ID exists in the document. issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs have broken aria-describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: Each form input's aria-describedby references an error ID (contact-name-error, contact-email-error, contact-subject-error, contact-message-error) that does not exist. The FormGroup component expects errorId and errorMessage props which are not passed, so error message elements are never rendered with those IDs.

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The btn-spinner span is purely decorative (the loading state is announced via VisuallyHidden text) but lacks aria-hidden="true". Some screen readers may attempt to announce the spinner element.

issuesFound.title: Spinner component ring element missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner__ring span is decorative (the parent has role="status" and aria-label) but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may attempt to announce the decorative ring element.

issuesFound.title: Trust badge star character not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The star character (★) in the G2 rating trust badge is decorative visual content that will be announced by screen readers (potentially as "black star" or "star") alongside the aria-label, creating redundant output.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle icon-only button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton with only an icon (emoji) as content, but does not pass the required aria-label prop. The IconButton interface requires aria-label for icon-only buttons, but ThemeToggle omits it, resulting in a button with no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 700: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation panel contains only the "✕" character as visual content but lacks the required aria-label prop. Screen reader users will hear only "button" with no indication of its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 608: `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle buttons missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that expand/collapse mobile navigation subsections have aria-controls pointing to their associated panels, but lack aria-expanded to communicate the current expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 644: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute incorrectly placed on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog". aria-modal indicates to assistive technologies that content outside the dialog is not interactive, and must be on the dialog element itself.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1665: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>`
  Fix: Line 1668: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`
